1228	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1229	[Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate]
1230	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1231	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1232	  % NOTE (iter-003): tensor-order convention. The Lean uses Mathlib's `pullbackSpecIso`
1233	  % orientation `A ⊗[R] R'` (so the codomain is `(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` and the generator
1234	  % goes `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`); the prose below writes `R' ⊗_R A`. The two are the
1235	  % same module up to `TensorProduct.comm` — a faithful re-orientation, no content change.
1236	  \uses{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib, lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}
1237	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1238	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
1239	  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
1240	  % L933--938).
1241	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
1242	  % equality
1243	  % $$
1244	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
1245	  % $$
1246	  % as $R'$-modules."
1247	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
1248	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1249	  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be ring homomorphisms and \(M\) an
1250	  \(A\)-module. There is a bundled \(R'\)-linear isomorphism
1251	  \[
1252	    \operatorname{regroup} :
1253	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M ,
1254	  \]
1255	  the ``equality as \(R'\)-modules'' of the source, sending the generator
1256	  \((r' \otimes 1) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\); equivalently its inverse sends
1257	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). It carries \emph{no} flatness
1258	  hypothesis.
1259	\end{lemma}
1260	
1261	\begin{proof}
1262	  \uses{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib, lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}
1263	  The object-level \(\texttt{ModuleCat}\) isomorphism is obtained directly from the
1264	  standalone \(R'\)-linear equivalence of
1265	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv} by
1266	  \(\texttt{LinearEquiv.toModuleIso}\); the separate compilation unit normalises the
1267	  \(\texttt{Module } A\,(A \otimes_R R')\) instance diamond so the bundled
1268	  \(\texttt{map\_smul'}\) discharges without the carrier-instance obstruction.
1269	  % NOTE (iter-006 review): this proof prescription is UNSOUND for the current Mathlib
1270	  % pin and must be corrected by a blueprint-writer before re-dispatch. The deployed
1271	  % file-split (refactor `split-regroup`) did NOT let
1272	  % `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso (base_change_regroup_linearEquiv ↑M)` close `map_smul'`:
1273	  % the helper's source `(A⊗_R R')⊗_A M` (canonical leftAlgebra A-action) and the object
1274	  % carrier `(extendScalars includeLeftRingHom).obj M` (restrictScalars A-action) are
1275	  % DIFFERENT TYPES — the A-module is a `TensorProduct` instance arg, so separate
1276	  % compilation normalises the value-level diamond but never the tensor TYPE. The `eT`
1277	  % identity-A-linear bridge is essential. The real residue is an opaque-instance wall on
1278	  % the `letI := inferInstanceAs (Module R' …)` carriers (no `SMul*` synthesis through the
1279	  % aux-def); the fix is a TRANSPARENT `Module R'` instance / project-local `ModuleCat`
1280	  % base-change iso for the `restrictScalars∘extendScalars` square, then the reduction chain
1281	  % `restrictScalars.smul_def → ExtendScalars.smul_tmul → tmul_mul_tmul → (helper simp set)`.
1282	  % See task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-fbc-iter006.md (must-fix) and session_6.
1283	  This is pure tensor algebra, assembled entirely from Mathlib's heterobasic
1284	  equivalences with no geometry. Work in the convention with order
1285	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) (the same module as \(R' \otimes_R A\) up to
1286	  \(\texttt{TensorProduct.comm}\), per the convention note):
1287	  \[
1288	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M
1289	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \texttt{comm}\ }\; M \otimes_A (A \otimes_R R')
1290	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\ }\; M \otimes_R R'
1291	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \texttt{comm}\ }\; R' \otimes_R M .
1292	  \]
1293	  The first and last arrows are the \(R'\)-linear heterobasic commutativity
1294	  isomorphisms \(\texttt{TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.comm}\) (the \(R'\)-action
1295	  enters through the \(A \otimes_R R'\) factor, so the \(R'\)-linear versions apply); the
1296	  middle arrow is \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) of
1297	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}, instantiated at the tower \(R \to A \to A\)
1298	  with module \(M\) and base-change object \(R'\). On the generator the composite acts as
1299	  \[
1300	    (1 \otimes r') \otimes m \;\mapsto\; m \otimes (1 \otimes r')
1301	    \;\xmapsto{\ \operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\ }\; (1 \cdot m) \otimes r'
1302	    = m \otimes r' \;\mapsto\; r' \otimes m ,
1303	  \]
1304	  using \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}(m \otimes_A (1 \otimes_R r')) = (1 \cdot m)
1305	  \otimes_R r'\) (\(\texttt{cancelBaseChange\_tmul}\)). In the prose orientation this is
1306	  \((r' \otimes 1) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\). Being a composite of linear
1307	  equivalences it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of the three factors carries
1308	  a flatness hypothesis.
1309	\end{proof}
1310	
1311	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1312	[Generator identification of the conjugated section-level map]
1313	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace_eq}
1314	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_generator_trace_eq}
1315	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
1316	        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1317	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1318	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
1319	  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
1320	  % L933--938).
1321	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
1322	  % equality
1323	  % $$
1324	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
1325	  % $$
1326	  % as $R'$-modules."
1327	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
1328	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1329	  With the domain and codomain pinned by Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}
1330	  and Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}, the conjugated section-level
1331	  base-change map
1332	  \[
1333	    \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\alpha) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} :
1334	    R' \otimes_R M \longrightarrow (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M
1335	  \]
1336	  sends \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\); equivalently it equals the
1337	  \emph{inverse} of the regrouping isomorphism
1338	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}, i.e.\
1339	  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\alpha) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1}
1340	  = \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\).
1341	\end{lemma}
1342	
1343	\begin{proof}
1344	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
1345	        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1346	  By Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}, \(\alpha\) is the
1347	  \((g^* \dashv g_*)\)-transpose of \(f_*(\eta')\), with \(\eta'\) the
1348	  \(((g')^*,(g')_*)\)-unit. We trace a generator through the three pieces, reading each
1349	  functor on global modules via the dictionaries of
1350	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read} and
1351	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.
1352	  \begin{itemize}
1353	    \item[(1)] \emph{The \(((g')^*,(g')_*)\)-unit \(\eta'\).} Under the dictionaries
1354	      \((g')^* = (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A (-)\) (extension of scalars along
1355	      \(A \to R' \otimes_R A\)) and \((g')_*\) is restriction back along it; the unit
1356	      on the global \(A\)-module \(M\) is the base-change unit
1357	      \(m \mapsto 1 \otimes m = (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\).
1358	    \item[(2)] \emph{Apply \(f_* = \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\) and reindex.}
1359	      Restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\) leaves the assignment
1360	      \(m \mapsto 1 \otimes m\) unchanged on elements; the pseudofunctor identities
1361	      \((g'f)_* = (f'g)_* = g_* f'_*\) reindex the target as the \(R'\)-module
1362	      \(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M = (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) of
1363	      Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.
1364	    \item[(3)] \emph{Transpose under \((g^* \dashv g_*)\) for \(\psi\).} The transpose
1365	      of an \(R\)-linear map \(u : \operatorname{restr}_\varphi M \to
1366	      \operatorname{restr}_\psi N\) is the unique \(R'\)-linear
1367	      \(\widehat u : R' \otimes_R M \to N\) with
1368	      \(\widehat u(r' \otimes m) = r' \cdot u(m)\); the source reads as
1369	      \(R' \otimes_R M\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}). Hence
1370	      \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \bigl((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\bigr)
1371	      = (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\), using that the \(R'\)-action scales the
1372	      \(R'\)-factor.
1373	  \end{itemize}
1374	  This assignment \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\) is exactly the
1375	  inverse generator of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}, so the conjugated
1376	  map agrees with \(\operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\) on generators and hence (both being
1377	  \(R'\)-linear) everywhere. This is exactly the ``boils down to the equality''
1378	  computation of the source.
1379	\end{proof}
1380	
1381	\begin{lemma}
1382	
1383	
1384	\leanok
1385	[Generator trace of the section-level base-change map]
1386	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace}
1387	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_generator_trace}
1388	  % NOTE (iter-003): the Lean decl formalizes the `IsIso (Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(α) ≫ Θ_tgt)`
1389	  % *corollary* of this lemma, not the literal generator formula
1390	  % `r' ⊗ m ↦ (r' ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`. The IsIso form is what the parent
1391	  % lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange consumes; it is faithful and
1392	  % non-vacuous. With the generator identification
1393	  % (lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace_eq) the residual obstacle is gone: the
1394	  % conjugate equals a LinearEquiv (regroupEquiv.symm), so this is a one-line
1395	  % `rw` + `infer_instance`.
1396	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace_eq}
1397	  With the domain and codomain pinned by Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}
1398	  and Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}, the conjugated section-level
1399	  base-change map
1400	  \[
1401	    \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} \;\ggg\; \Gamma(\alpha) \;\ggg\; \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} :
1402	    R' \otimes_R M \longrightarrow (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M
1403	  \]
1404	  is an isomorphism: \(\mathrm{IsIso}\bigl(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} \ggg \Gamma(\alpha)
1405	  \ggg \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\bigr)\).
1406	\end{lemma}
1407	
1408	\begin{proof}
1409	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace_eq}
1410	  By the generator identification Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace_eq}
1411	  the conjugated map equals \(\operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\), the inverse of the bundled
1412	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}. The inverse
1413	  of a linear equivalence is itself an isomorphism, so
1414	  \(\mathrm{IsIso}\bigl(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} \ggg \Gamma(\alpha)
1415	  \ggg \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\bigr)\) follows since \(\operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\)
1416	  is a linear equivalence and hence an isomorphism.
1417	\end{proof}
1418	
1419	\begin{lemma}
1420	
1421	\leanok
1422	[Section-level value of the base-change map equals the cancellation isomorphism]
1423	  \label{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}
1424	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}

350	  \(k[X_1, \dots, X_s] \to R\) that is module-finite, i.e.\ \(R\) is a finite module
351	  over a polynomial subalgebra on algebraically independent generators.
352	\end{lemma}
353	
354	\begin{lemma}\leanok
355	[L4 — Noether normalisation with clearing of denominators]
356	  \label{lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators}
357	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial}
358	  \uses{lem:noether_normalization_fg}
359	  % NOTE (resolved iter-006): the LEAN SIGNATURE block below now matches the landed
360	  % Lean decl `exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial`, which RETAINS the binder
361	  % `(_ : Algebra (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))` as
362	  % the 3rd existential (after `(_ : g ≠ 0)`, before `φ`) — required to type the
363	  % AlgHom target as an A_g-algebra. The MATH statement is unaffected.
364	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (landed target, AlgHom encoding). Write
365	  %   A_g := Localization.Away g,   B_g := Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g).
366	  % The conclusion is:
367	  %   ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
368	  %     (_ : Algebra (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))
369	  %     (φ : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)
370	  %           →ₐ[Localization.Away g] Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)),
371	  %     Function.Injective φ ∧
372	  %     (letI := φ.toAlgebra;
373	  %      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g))
374	  %                    (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))
375	  % The polynomial subalgebra A_g[b_1,…,b_n] is realised as the DOMAIN
376	  % `MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g`, embedded into B_g by the algebra map φ; the
377	  % generators b_j are φ(X_j). Compared with an earlier draft using three anonymous
378	  % instance existentials (∃ (_ : Algebra A_g B_g) (_ : Algebra (MvPoly … A_g) B_g)
379	  % (_ : IsScalarTower …)), this form keeps only the single `Algebra A_g B_g` binder
380	  % needed to type B_g as an A_g-algebra: the single φ then carries the algebra map,
381	  % `Function.Injective φ` encodes algebraic independence of the b_j, and
382	  % `Module.Finite … over φ.toAlgebra` encodes module-finiteness of B_g over the
383	  % image polynomial subalgebra. Downstream, L5 (\cref{lem:gf_polynomial_core})
384	  % consumes a finite module over `MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g`; the AlgHom hands it
385	  % exactly that algebra structure via `φ.toAlgebra`, with no instance revival.
386	  % Why B_g and not B: generic flatness only needs the property after one
387	  % inversion g; the integral-dependence equations only clear denominators after
388	  % inverting g (see the SOURCE QUOTE PROOF below).
389	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, normalisation step
390	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1747-L1758).
391	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
392	  % "By Noether normalisation lemma, there exist elements $b_1,\ldots,b_n \in B$,
393	  %  such that $K\otimes_AB$ is finite over its subalgebra $K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$ and
394	  %  the elements $b_1,\ldots, b_n$ are algebraically independent over $K$. [...]
395	  %  If $g\ne 0$ in $A$ is chosen to be a `common denominator' for coefficients of
396	  %  equations of integral dependence satisfied by a finite set of algebra
397	  %  generators for $K\otimes_AB$ over $K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$, we see that $B_g$ is
398	  %  finite over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$."
399	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (Noether
400	  normalisation with denominators).}
401	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain with fraction field \(K\) and \(B\) a finite-type
402	  \(A\)-algebra that is a domain with \(B_K = K \otimes_A B \ne 0\). Write
403	  \(A_g = \texttt{Localization.Away}~g\) and
404	  \(B_g = \texttt{Localization.Away (algebraMap } A\,B\,g)\) for the localisations
405	  away from \(g\) and its image. Then there exist a natural number \(n\), an element
406	  \(g \in A\) with \(g \ne 0\), and an injective \(A_g\)-algebra homomorphism
407	  \[
408	    \varphi : A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \longrightarrow B_g,
409	  \]
410	  where \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] = \texttt{MvPolynomial (Fin } n)~A_g\) is the
411	  polynomial ring on \(n\) algebraically independent variables, such that \(B_g\) is
412	  module-finite over the image polynomial subalgebra via \(\varphi\). The injectivity
413	  of \(\varphi\) records the algebraic independence of the generators
414	  \(b_j := \varphi(X_j)\), and the module-finiteness is taken over the algebra
415	  structure that \(\varphi\) puts on \(B_g\). Equivalently, the classical statement
416	  ``\(B_g\) is finite over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\)'' is encoded by the single map
417	  \(\varphi\): it both names the polynomial subalgebra (as the domain
418	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n]\)) and carries the algebra structure under which \(B_g\) is
419	  finite.
420	\end{lemma}
421	
422	\begin{proof}
423	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1747-L1758).
424	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (Noether normalisation with denominators).}
425	  Apply Noether normalisation over the field \(K\)
426	  (\cref{lem:noether_normalization_fg}) to the finite-type \(K\)-algebra
427	  \(B_K = K \otimes_A B\): there are algebraically independent
428	  \(\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n \in B_K\) with \(B_K\) module-finite over
429	  \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\). Clearing denominators, each \(\bar b_j\) is
430	  represented by some \(b_j \in B\) (writing \(1 \otimes b\) as \(b\)). Fix a finite
431	  set of algebra generators of \(B_K\) over \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\) and
432	  finitely many equations of integral dependence they satisfy; let \(g \in A\),
433	  \(g \ne 0\), be a common denominator of the (finitely many) coefficients of those
434	  equations. After inverting \(g\) all these integrality relations hold over
435	  \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\), and the chosen generators generate \(B_g\) as a module
436	  over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\); hence \(B_g\) is module-finite over
437	  \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\). The algebra homomorphism \(\varphi\) of the statement is
438	  the \(A_g\)-algebra map \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \to B_g\) sending \(X_j \mapsto b_j\);
439	  the algebraic independence of the \(b_j\) over \(A_g\) (inherited from their
440	  independence over \(K\)) makes \(\varphi\) injective, and the module-finiteness just
441	  established is exactly module-finiteness of \(B_g\) over the image of \(\varphi\).
442	\end{proof}
443	
444	\begin{lemma}\leanok
445	[L5 — polynomial-ring core of generic freeness]
446	  \label{lem:gf_polynomial_core}
447	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial}
448	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module, lem:gf_splice_shortExact, lem:gf_torsion_base}
449	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, inductive step
450	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1760-L1772).
451	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
452	  % "Let $m$ be the generic rank of the finite module $B_g$ over the domain
453	  %  $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$. Then we have a short exact sequence of
454	  %  $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$-modules of the form
455	  %  $0\to A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]^{\oplus m} \to B_g \to T \to 0$ where $T$ is a finite
456	  %  torsion module over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$. Therefore, the dimension of the
457	  %  support of $K\otimes_{A_g}T$ as a $K\otimes_{A_g}(B_g)$-module is strictly less
458	  %  than $n$. Hence by induction on $n$ (applied to the data $A_g$, $B_g$, $T$),
459	  %  there exists some $h\ne 0$ in $A$ with $T_h$ free over $A_{gh}$. Taking $f=gh$,
460	  %  the lemma follows from the above short exact sequence."
461	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (polynomial-ring
462	  core of the induction).}
463	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain and \(d \ge 0\). Let \(N\) be a finite module over
464	  the polynomial ring \(A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\), regarded as an \(A\)-module. Then there
465	  exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that \(N_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
466	
467	  This is the genuine Mathlib-absent residue of generic freeness: the bottom of the
468	  Nitsure \S4 induction, on which the whole finite-type case rests.
469	\end{lemma}
470	
471	\begin{proof}
472	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1760-L1772).
473	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (polynomial-ring core).}
474	  We induct on \(d\). For \(d = 0\) the ring is \(A\) itself and \(N\) is a finite
475	  \(A\)-module, so the claim is exactly the finite-module leaf
476	  \cref{lem:gf_finite_module}. For \(d \ge 1\), pass to \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\) and
477	  set \(N_K = K \otimes_A N\). If \(N_K = 0\) the torsion base case
478	  \cref{lem:gf_torsion_base} already produces \(f \ne 0\) with \(N_f = 0\) free.
479	  Otherwise let \(m\) be the generic rank of \(N\) over the domain
480	  \(A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\); choosing \(m\) elements of \(N\) whose images form a
481	  \(K(X_1, \dots, X_d)\)-basis of \(N_K\) and clearing denominators gives, after
482	  inverting some \(g \ne 0\) in \(A\), a short exact sequence of
483	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_d]\)-modules
484	  \[
485	    0 \to A_g[X_1, \dots, X_d]^{\oplus m} \to N_g \to T \to 0
486	  \]
487	  with \(T\) a finite torsion \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_d]\)-module. Because \(T\) is
488	  torsion, its support over \(K[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) has dimension \(\le d - 1\), so
489	  \(T\) is finite over a polynomial ring in fewer variables after a further
490	  inversion; the inductive hypothesis yields \(h \ne 0\) with \(T_h\) free over
491	  \(A_{gh}\). The free middle term \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_d]^{\oplus m}\) localises to a
492	  free \(A_{gh}\)-module by the \(d = 0\) leaf applied coordinatewise, and
493	  \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact} splices the two ends of the localised sequence to
494	  give \(N_f\) free over \(A_f\) with \(f := gh\).
495	\end{proof}

R'-linear equivalence `base_change_regroup_linearEquiv` by `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso`; the separate
compilation unit normalises the `Module A (A ⊗_R R')` instance diamond so the bundled `map_smul'`
discharges." **This prescription is mathematically UNSOUND for the current Mathlib pin and was
definitively refuted by the iter-006 prover.** The refutation (keep this reasoning; encode it as the

  `Module A` diamond but NOT the two tensor TYPES. Hence the identity-A-linear bridge `eT` between
  the two A-actions is mathematically essential; it cannot be eliminated.

identified (lemmas all confirmed present in Mathlib at this pin): prove `map_smul'` by

so the reduction chain fires:

    → `ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.smul_tmul` (`s • (s' ⊗ₜ m) = (s*s') ⊗ₜ m` on the extendScalars carrier)
    → `Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul` (`(1⊗r')*(a⊗s) = a⊗(r'*s)`)
    → then the helper's `comm` / `cancelBaseChange_tmul` / `comm` simp set to land on `r' ⊗ m`.

named (pick the one you judge cleaner and present it as primary, mention the other as a fallback):
  (a) a `TensorProduct.ext`-style linearity check that keeps the generator `(a ⊗ₜ s) ⊗ₜ m` typed at

The monolithic per-generator identity `(Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(α) ≫ Θ_tgt)(1 ⊗ x) = regroupEquiv.inv (1 ⊗ x)`

# Mathlib-analogist directive — GF generic-rank-SES Lean API (slug: gf-genrank)

## Mode: api-alignment

## Background
Project file `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean`, namespace `GenericFreeness`.
We are formalizing the polynomial-ring core of generic freeness (Nitsure §4), Lean decl
`exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial`:

  Let `A` be a noetherian domain, `d ≥ 0`, and `N` a finite module over `P_d := MvPolynomial (Fin d) A`,
  also an `A`-module with `IsScalarTower A P_d N`. Then ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
  `Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N)`.

The proof is strong induction on `d` (`Nat.strong_induction_on generalizing N` — already landed). The
NON-TORSION inductive step is the Mathlib-absent residue. Following Nitsure §4 verbatim:

  Let `m` = generic rank of `N` over the domain `P_d = A[X_1,…,X_d]`. There is a short exact sequence
  of `P_d`-modules  `0 → P_d^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0`  (after inverting some `g ≠ 0` in `A`), with `T` a
  finite torsion `P_d`-module. The support of `K ⊗ T` (K = Frac A) has dimension `< d`, so `T` is
  finite over a polynomial ring in fewer variables after a further inversion; the IH applied to `T`
  gives `h ≠ 0` with `T_h` free over `A_{gh}`; splicing the SES (already-proved L3
  `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact`) gives `N_f` free over `A_f`, `f = gh`.

## The decision I need you to align (this is the named STUCK-risk for the route)
The iter-006 prover stopped short of stubbing the sub-lemmas because "exact type signatures depend on
the generic-rank API one chooses." I need the Mathlib-idiomatic Lean encoding for THREE things, so a
blueprint-writer can pin concretely-typed sub-lemma signatures that a prover can actually formalize:

1. **Generic rank.** How does Mathlib idiomatically express "the generic rank of a finite module `N`
   over a polynomial ring `P_d = MvPolynomial (Fin d) A` (A a noetherian domain)"? Candidates to
   assess: `Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N)`; or
   `Module.rank`; or base-changing `N` to `FractionRing P_d` via `TensorProduct`. Which gives the
   cleanest path to "choose `m` elements of `N` whose images are a basis of `N ⊗ Frac(P_d)`"? Is there
   a Mathlib lemma giving a basis / linear independence of lifted elements (e.g. `Basis`, 
   `Module.Free.chooseBasis`, `FiniteDimensional` over the fraction field, `Module.finBasis`)?

2. **The generic-rank SES `0 → P_d^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0`.** What is the Mathlib idiom for: choosing `m`
   elements of `N`, building the `P_d`-linear map `P_d^{⊕m} → N` (`Fintype.linearCombination` /
   `Finsupp.linearCombination` / `Basis.constr`), showing it is injective after inverting a suitable
   `g` (so the cokernel `T` is torsion), and defining `T` as the cokernel? Is there existing API for
   "a map of f.g. modules over a Noetherian domain that is injective after localization at the generic
   point becomes injective after inverting a single element" (denominator-clearing / `LocalizedModule`
   / `IsLocalizedModule` exactness)? Name the relevant `LocalizedModule` / `Submodule.span` /
   `Module.Finite` lemmas.

3. **Support-dimension-drop reindex of `T`.** `T` is a finite torsion `P_d`-module; we need it
   re-presented as a finite module over `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A` with `m' < d` so the IH (which
   universally quantifies the module type over `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A`) applies. What Mathlib API
   exists for: the annihilator of a torsion f.g. module over `P_d` being a nonzero ideal, Noether
   normalization of the quotient `P_d / Ann(T)` giving a module-finite polynomial subring in fewer
   variables (`MvPolynomial (Fin m') A`)? Relevant: `Module.support`, `Module.annihilator`,
   `Ideal.height`/`Ring.KrullDim`, `Algebra.IsIntegral`, Noether normalization
   (`exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg` / `MvPolynomial` normalization). Is the "dimension < d ⟹ finite
   over fewer variables" step available, or is it itself a project-side build?

Also re-confirm the existence/spelling of `induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime` (the
prime-filtration device named in STRATEGY.md) — the strategy-critic flagged it as a name to verify.

## Deliverable
For each of the three pieces: the Mathlib-idiomatic API path (named declarations, verified to exist
via lean search), OR an explicit "Mathlib-absent → project must build it" verdict with the closest
existing scaffolding. Recommend the concrete Lean SIGNATURE shape for the two sub-lemmas the
blueprint will pin: `gf_generic_rank_ses` (constructs the SES) and `gf_torsion_reindex` (re-presents
T over fewer variables). Write the persistent rationale to `analogies/gf-generic-rank-ses.md`.
EOF

34	for `p : PrimeSpectrum A`, (iii) short-exact `N₁ ↪ N₂ ↠ N₃` with motive on the ends ⟹ on `N₂`, gives

39	## Decisions identified
40	
41	### Decision 1: how to express the generic rank `m`
42	
43	- **Mathlib idiom**: `m := Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N)`.
44	  `P_d = MvPolynomial (Fin d) A` is a noetherian domain (`MvPolynomial.isDomain`, Hilbert basis), so
45	  `nonZeroDivisors P_d` localizes `N` to a module over `Localization (nonZeroDivisors P_d) = FractionRing P_d`,
46	  a finite-dimensional `FractionRing P_d`-vector space (finite, since `N` is finite over `P_d`).
47	  `Module.finrank` over a field is the right invariant; `Module.rank` (`Cardinal`-valued) is the wrong
48	  altitude — you want a `ℕ` to index `Fin m`.
49	- **Choosing `m` lifts of `N` whose images form a basis** — atoms all verified:
50	  - `Module.finBasis (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule … N) : Basis (Fin m) (FractionRing P_d) (…)`
51	    (`Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Free`) — the field-vector-space basis indexed by `Fin (finrank)`.
52	  - `IsLocalizedModule.surj (nonZeroDivisors P_d) (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap …)` — every basis vector
53	    is `(1/s) • (image of some n ∈ N)`; `s` a unit in the field, so the **images** `{mk n_i}` are again a
54	    basis (clear denominators). (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Basic`.)
55	  - `LinearIndependent.restrict_scalars` (P_d ↪ FractionRing P_d injective) descends linear independence
56	    of the lifts from `FractionRing P_d` back to `P_d` — this is what makes `φ` (below) injective.
57	  - Reverse helper `Module.Basis.ofIsLocalizedModule` / `LinearIndependent.localization`
58	    (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Module`) go R→Rₛ; useful for the spanning/surjectivity half.
59	- **Gap**: divergent-equivalent — Mathlib has every atom but **no single packaged "choose `m` elements
60	  whose images are a basis of the localization" lemma**. The number is idiomatic; the lift is project glue.
61	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB on the number (`Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule …)`),
62	  NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL on the lift (small, all anchors present).
63	
64	### Decision 2: encoding the generic-rank SES `gf_generic_rank_ses`
65	
66	- **Mathlib idiom for the map**: `φ := Fintype.linearCombination P_d v` where `v : Fin m → N` are the
67	  lifts of Decision 1 (`Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Finsupp.LinearCombination`). Domain `(Fin m → P_d)` **is**
68	  the concrete `P_d^{⊕m}` (matches the `M'` slot of L3 exactly). Companions: `Fintype.linearCombination_apply`
69	  (`∑ i, f i • v i`), `Fintype.range_linearCombination` (`range = span (Set.range v)`).
70	  `φ` injective ⟺ `LinearIndependent P_d v` (got from Decision 1 via `restrict_scalars`).
71	- **Cokernel / torsion**: `T := N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ`; the surjection is `Submodule.mkQ (range φ)`
72	  (surjective, and `Function.Exact φ (mkQ (range φ))` holds — `range φ = ker (mkQ)`). `T` torsion encoded
73	  as `Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T` (preferred — it is the literal input of the reindex's
74	  annihilator anchor) or equivalently `Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) T)` via
75	  `LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff`. Torsion holds because the images of `v` span the localization
76	  (basis), so `φ ⊗ FractionRing P_d` is surjective, hence `T ⊗ FractionRing P_d = 0`.
77	- **Important simplification vs the directive**: the SES is constructible over `P_d` **directly — no
78	  inversion of `g ∈ A` is needed for this step**. `φ` is injective on the nose (`ker φ` is torsion in the
79	  torsion-free `P_d`-module `P_d^{⊕m}`, hence `0`), and `T` is torsion on the nose. The `g` that Nitsure
80	  mentions belongs entirely to the *reindex* (Decision 3), not the SES. So `gf_generic_rank_ses` carries
81	  no `g`. (The "denominator-clearing exactness after inverting one element" API the directive asked about
82	  is therefore unnecessary here.)
83	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — no packaged generic-rank SES, but every atom exists and the assembly
84	  is short.
85	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (build `gf_generic_rank_ses` from the atoms above).
86	
87	### Decision 3: the torsion reindex `gf_torsion_reindex` — and the base-domain induction shape
88	
89	- **Nonzero annihilator (Mathlib ✓)**: `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors`
90	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Torsion.Basic`): a finite `Module.IsTorsion R M` over comm `R` has
91	  `(⊤.annihilator ∩ nonZeroDivisors R).Nonempty`. For the domain `P_d` this gives `0 ≠ F ∈ Ann_{P_d}(T)`.
92	- **Variable drop (Mathlib-ABSENT)**: from `0 ≠ F ∈ Ann(T)`, one wants `T` module-finite over a polynomial
93	  ring in `m' < d` variables. Cleanest route that **avoids Krull-dimension theory**: a Nagata change of
94	  variables makes `F` monic in `X_d` up to its leading coefficient `g ∈ A`; after inverting `g`,
95	  `A_g[X_1..X_d]/(F)` is module-finite (division algorithm) over `A_g[X_1..X_{d-1}] = MvPolynomial (Fin (d-1)) A_g`,
96	  and `T` (finite over `P_d/(F)`) is finite over it by `Module.Finite.trans`. So `m' = d-1 < d` — **no
97	  `ringKrullDim` needed**. Mathlib has the *field* Noether normalization `exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg`
98	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization`) and `ringKrullDim` theory (`Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.*`,
99	  e.g. `Polynomial.ringKrullDim_le`) but **not** the domain-level single-variable elimination with
100	  denominator clearing. This is the **same engine** the already-stubbed L4
101	  `exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial` needs — build once, reuse.
102	- **CRITICAL design consequence — the induction must generalize the base domain `A`.** The reindex inverts
103	  `g ∈ A`, so `T_g` is finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g)` — base ring `A_g`, **not**
104	  `A`. The IH of `exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial` as currently written fixes `A`, so
105	  `IH m' _ T_g` does **not typecheck** (wrong base ring). This is the real cause of the iter-006
106	  "signatures depend on the generic-rank API" stall: it is not the rank API, it is that the recursion
107	  changes the base. **Fix**: state/prove the core with the motive universally quantifying the noetherian
108	  domain — `∀ d, ∀ (A) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A] (N) […], ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Free A_f N_f`,
109	  by `Nat.strong_induction_on d` with `A` (and its three instances, and all of `N`'s `d`-dependent
110	  instances) reverted into the motive. `A` is already an explicit argument, so this is a reversion change,
111	  not a signature change to the public lemma. Apply `IH m' (hm' : m' < d) (Localization.Away g) T_g` at
112	  base `A_g` (still a noetherian domain: `IsLocalization.isDomain`/`Localization.Away` of a noeth domain).
113	- **Descent of the IH witness back to `A`**: IH gives `h ≠ 0` in `A_g` with `T_g` free over `(A_g)_h`.
114	  Since `A_g = Localization.Away g` and `h = a / g^k`, `(A_g)_h ≅ A_{g·a}` and `T_{g·a} ≅ (T_g)_h`, so the
115	  freeness descends to `f'' := g·a ∈ A`. This is the same localization-tower freeness transport as the
116	  already-proved L3b `free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul` (a thin bridge across `A → A_g → (A_g)_h`).
117	- **The `P_d^{⊕m}` end is free over `A_f` for free (Mathlib ✓)**: `MvPolynomial.instFree`
118	  (`Module.Free A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)`) + `Pi`/localization free, so the `M'` end of L3 is free over
119	  `A_{f'}` for *any* `f' ≠ 0`. (Note: the file's comment "applied coordinatewise via the d=0 leaf" is
120	  slightly off — `P_d^{⊕m}` is **not** module-finite over `A`, but it **is** free, which is all L3 needs.)
121	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (variable-drop engine + base-`A` generalization).
122	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL.
123	
124	## Recommended sub-lemma signatures (for the blueprint pin)
125	
126	```lean
127	/-- `gf_generic_rank_ses` — the generic-rank short exact sequence. No `g`: built over `P_d` directly. -/
128	theorem gf_generic_rank_ses
129	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
130	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N]
131	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
132	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
133	    ∃ (m : ℕ) (φ : (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin d) A] N),
134	      Function.Injective φ ∧
135	      Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ)
136	-- witness: m = finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N);
137	--          φ = Fintype.linearCombination P_d v, v the denominator-cleared basis lifts.
138	```
139	
140	```lean
141	/-- `gf_torsion_reindex` — re-present a finite torsion `P_d`-module over fewer variables after
142	inverting `g ∈ A`. Mirrors the existential style of the already-present L4
143	`exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial`. -/
144	theorem gf_torsion_reindex
145	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
146	    (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T]
147	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
148	    [Module A T] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
149	    (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
150	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0) (m' : ℕ) (_ : m' < d)
151	      (_ : Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
152	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
153	      (_ : Module (Localization.Away g) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
154	      (_ : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g)
155	              (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
156	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)),
157	      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
158	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)
159	-- can fix m' = d - 1 (single-variable elimination); strong induction accepts any m' < d.
160	```
161	
162	And the **core's proof must be restructured** to generalize `A`:
163	```lean
164	-- helper, then `exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial` is its specialization:
165	∀ d, ∀ (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
166	       (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
167	       [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module A N]
168	       [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N],
169	     ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N)
170	-- proved by `Nat.strong_induction_on d`, A reverted into the motive.
171	```
172	
173	## Recommendation
174	Pin `m := Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N)` as the generic
175	rank — idiomatic and `ℕ`-valued. Build `gf_generic_rank_ses` (no `g`) from `Fintype.linearCombination`
176	on denominator-cleared basis lifts (`Module.finBasis` + `IsLocalizedModule.surj` +
177	`LinearIndependent.restrict_scalars`), with `T := N ⧸ range φ` and `Module.IsTorsion P_d T`. Build
178	`gf_torsion_reindex` on the L4 existential template, using `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors`
179	for `0 ≠ F ∈ Ann(T)` and the **project-side** Nagata single-variable elimination + denominator clearing
180	(shared with L4) to land over `MvPolynomial (Fin (d-1)) (Localization.Away g)`. **The load-bearing fix is
181	structural**: generalize the base domain `A` in the `Nat.strong_induction_on`, because the reindex changes
182	the base ring to `A_g` and the IH must be available there. None of these require weakening any pinned type.
183

non-torsion generic-rank dévissage (`lem:gf_polynomial_core`) and the L4 denominator-clearing
(`lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators`) — are monolithic Mathlib-absent constructions. The progress-
critic returned CHURNING (GF at the STUCK boundary); the named corrective is to DECOMPOSE both into
named, CONCRETELY-TYPED sub-lemmas so an iter-008 prover can formalize provable pieces. A
mathlib-analogist (api-alignment) has already resolved the Lean encoding — its findings are
authoritative for this directive and are reproduced inline below. The persistent rationale is at
`analogies/gf-generic-rank-ses.md` (read it; it has the verified Mathlib atoms and the recommended
signatures).

## CRITICAL structural finding (load-bearing — this is the real cause of the iter-006 stall)
The analogist found that the iter-006 "strong induction generalizing N" skeleton is NECESSARY but
INSUFFICIENT. The torsion reindex inverts an element `g ∈ A`, so the reindexed module `T_g` is finite
over `MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g)` — base ring `A_g`, NOT `A`. The IH of
`exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial` as currently written fixes `A`, so `IH m' _ T_g` does NOT
typecheck (wrong base ring). **The induction must ALSO generalize the base noetherian domain `A`.**
This must be recorded in the blueprint so the iter-008 prover restructures L5 accordingly. `A` is
already an explicit argument, so this is a reversion-into-the-motive change, not a public-signature
change. (A_g remains a noetherian domain: `IsLocalization`/`Localization.Away` of a noetherian domain.)

## TASK 1 — Add two new sub-lemma blocks before `lem:gf_polynomial_core` (statement at L444–469;
proof L471–495). Use these EXACT analogist-recommended signatures as the Lean pins.

### Sub-lemma `lem:gf_generic_rank_ses` — the generic-rank short exact sequence (NO `g`-inversion).
`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.gf_generic_rank_ses}`. Statement: for `A` a noetherian
domain, `d : ℕ`, `N` finite over `P_d := MvPolynomial (Fin d) A` (with the `A`-module +
`IsScalarTower A P_d N` structure), there exist `m : ℕ` and a `P_d`-linear
`φ : (Fin m → P_d) →ₗ[P_d] N` with `φ` injective and `N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ` torsion
(`Module.IsTorsion P_d (N ⧸ range φ)`).
- Informal proof: `m := Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N)`
  (the generic rank — `ℕ`-valued, idiomatic). Take `v : Fin m → N` lifting a `FractionRing P_d`-basis
  of `LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N` (basis from `Module.finBasis`; lift via
  `IsLocalizedModule.surj` clearing the unit denominators; `LinearIndependent.restrict_scalars`
  descends independence to `P_d`, making `φ := Fintype.linearCombination P_d v` injective).
  `T := N ⧸ range φ` is torsion because `v`'s images form a basis of the localization, so
  `φ ⊗ FractionRing P_d` is surjective and `T ⊗ FractionRing P_d = 0`. Emphasize in prose: the SES
  lives over `P_d` directly — no inversion of any `g ∈ A` is needed at this step (Nitsure's `g`
  belongs entirely to the reindex).
- `\uses{}`: the finite-rank/localization machinery is Mathlib (no project labels), so `\uses{}` may
  be empty or reference only Mathlib anchors if you add any. Do NOT invent project labels.
- SOURCE: this is the SES `0 → A_g[b]^{⊕m} → B_g → T → 0` of the Nitsure §4 quote already in the
  chapter (the L5 SOURCE QUOTE PROOF, nitsure tex L1760–1772). Reuse that citation pointer; you may
  read `references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex` to re-confirm the verbatim text.

### Sub-lemma `lem:gf_torsion_reindex` — re-present a finite torsion `P_d`-module over fewer variables.
`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.gf_torsion_reindex}`. Use the analogist's existential
signature (it mirrors the L4 existential template): for `A` noetherian domain, `0 < d`, `T` finite
over `P_d` with `Module.IsTorsion P_d T`, there exist `g : A`, `g ≠ 0`, `m' < d`, and the
`MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g)`-module + `Localization.Away g`-module +
`IsScalarTower` structure on `LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T`, such that that localized
module is `Module.Finite` over `MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g)`. (Copy the exact binder
list from `analogies/gf-generic-rank-ses.md` § "Recommended sub-lemma signatures".)
- Informal proof: `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors` gives `0 ≠ F ∈ Ann_{P_d}(T)`.
  Nagata change of variables makes `F` monic in `X_d` up to a leading coefficient `g ∈ A`; after
  inverting `g`, `A_g[X_1..X_d]/(F)` is module-finite (division algorithm) over
  `A_g[X_1..X_{d-1}] = MvPolynomial (Fin (d-1)) A_g`, and `T_g` (finite over `P_d/(F)`) is finite over
  it by `Module.Finite.trans`. Take `m' = d - 1` — NO `ringKrullDim` theory needed.
- SHARED ENGINE NOTE: record (as a `% NOTE:`) that this single-variable Nagata elimination +
  denominator-clearing is the SAME engine the L4 `lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators` needs — it
  should be built once and reused. (See TASK 3.)
- SOURCE: same Nitsure §4 quote (the "dimension of support … strictly less than n … by induction"
  clause). Reuse the pointer.

## TASK 2 — Rewrite the proof of `lem:gf_polynomial_core` (L5) as a thin assembly over the two new
sub-lemmas, and add the base-domain-generalization structural note.
- Keep the statement block unchanged (signature + SOURCE QUOTE PROOF are correct).
- Rewrite the proof body to: induct on `d` (strong induction) GENERALIZING BOTH `A` AND `N`; `d=0`
  leaf = `lem:gf_finite_module`; `d≥1` torsion sub-case (`N_K=0`) = `lem:gf_torsion_base`; non-torsion
  step: apply `lem:gf_generic_rank_ses` to get `0 → P_d^{⊕m} → N → T → 0` with `T` torsion, then
  `lem:gf_torsion_reindex` to get `g ≠ 0`, `m' < d`, `T_g` finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A_g`;
  apply the IH AT BASE `A_g` (this is why `A` must be generalized) to get `h ≠ 0` in `A_g` with
  `(T_g)_h` free over `(A_g)_h`; descend the witness back to `A` via the localization-tower transport
  of `lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport` (L3b) — `(A_g)_h ≅ A_{g·a}` for `h = a/g^k`; the free
  end `P_d^{⊕m}` localizes free over any `A_{f'}` (it is `Module.Free A (MvPolynomial …)` —
  `MvPolynomial.instFree` — note it is NOT module-finite over `A`, but free is all `lem:gf_splice_shortExact`
  needs); finally `lem:gf_splice_shortExact` (L3) splices to give `N_f` free, `f := g·h`-descended.
- Add a `% LEAN PROOF STRUCTURE (iter-007):` comment block stating the induction must generalize the
  base domain `A` (revert `A` + its `CommRing/IsDomain/IsNoetherianRing` instances into the motive
  alongside `N` and its `d`-dependent instances), because the reindex changes the base ring to `A_g`
  and `IH m' _ (Localization.Away g) T_g` must typecheck at base `A_g`. Cite `analogies/gf-generic-rank-ses.md`.
- Update the proof `\uses{}` to add `lem:gf_generic_rank_ses, lem:gf_torsion_reindex` (keep
  `lem:gf_finite_module, lem:gf_splice_shortExact, lem:gf_torsion_base, lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport`).

into named sub-lemmas with concrete types.
- Add `lem:gf_clear_one_denominator`: a single integral-dependence equation over `K[b_1..b_n]` clears
  to hold over `A_g[b_1..b_n]` after inverting one common denominator `g` of its (finitely many)

- Rewrite the L4 proof to invoke `gf_clear_one_denominator` + the Finset-fold, keeping the existing
  Noether-normalisation-over-K Step 1 and the final AlgHom construction.
- Cross-reference the SHARED ENGINE: add a `% NOTE:` that `gf_clear_one_denominator`'s denominator-

  "clear finitely many denominators into one `Localization.Away g`" helper, both consume it.
- Keep the existing Nitsure §4 SOURCE/SOURCE QUOTE PROOF blocks verbatim.

## References
You MAY read `references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex` (§4, ~L1711–1772) to
re-confirm verbatim quotes for the new sub-lemma SOURCE anchors. Do not fabricate citations.

1261	\begin{proof}
1262	  \uses{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib, lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}
1263	  The underlying additive bijection is supplied by the standalone, geometry-free
1264	  \(R'\)-linear equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}; the work
1265	  of this proof is to repackage that equivalence as a morphism of \(R'\)-module objects.
1266	  Two points of bookkeeping make the repackaging non-trivial, and pinning them down
1267	  \emph{is} the content of the proof. We work in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation
1268	  (the same module as \(R' \otimes_R A\) up to the canonical commutativity isomorphism,
1269	  per the tensor-order convention note).
1270	
1271	  \emph{The two \(A\)-actions are distinct types, so an identity bridge is mandatory.}
1272	  The source of the standalone equivalence, \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), is the
1273	  relative tensor product over the \emph{canonical} \(A\)-action on \(A \otimes_R R'\)
1274	  (multiplication in the left factor), whereas the
1275	  carrier object produced by the dictionaries is
1276	  \((\operatorname{extendScalars}\iota)\,M\) with \(\iota : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) the
1277	  left inclusion, whose relative tensor product is taken over the
1278	  \(\operatorname{restr}_\iota\) \(A\)-action. These two \(A\)-module structures on
1279	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) coincide on elements, but the tensor product depends on the
1280	  \(A\)-module structure in its type, so the two tensor products are \emph{not equal}
1281	  even though their underlying abelian groups are the same. One therefore inserts the identity \(A\)-linear bridge
1282	  \[
1283	    eT : (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\mathrm{can}} M
1284	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1285	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\operatorname{restr}} M ,
1286	  \]
1287	  which re-reads the same generators against the carrier's \(A\)-action. This bridge is
1288	  the mathematically essential glue between the standalone helper and the object; it
1289	  cannot be elided.
1290	
1291	  \emph{The residue is the \(R'\)-linearity of the assembled additive map, checked on
1292	  generators.} After the bridge, the only remaining obligation is that the assembled
1293	  additive equivalence respects the \(R'\)-module structures. The \(R'\)-action on the
1294	  carrier is restriction of scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\),
1295	  \(r' \mapsto 1 \otimes r'\), so \(r' \cdot y = (1 \otimes r') \cdot y\). On a
1296	  generator the extension-of-scalars action then computes
1297	  \[
1298	    (1 \otimes r') \cdot \bigl((a \otimes s) \otimes m\bigr)
1299	    = \bigl((1 \otimes r')(a \otimes s)\bigr) \otimes m
1300	    = \bigl(a \otimes (r' s)\bigr) \otimes m ,
1301	  \]
1302	  using \((1 \otimes r')(a \otimes s) = a \otimes (r' s)\) in \(A \otimes_R R'\). The
1303	  helper's underlying map sends \((a \otimes s) \otimes m \mapsto s \otimes (a \cdot m)\)
1304	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}), so it carries
1305	  \(\bigl(a \otimes (r' s)\bigr) \otimes m\) to
1306	  \((r' s) \otimes (a \cdot m) = r' \cdot \bigl(s \otimes (a \cdot m)\bigr)\), which is
1307	  \(r'\) times the image of the original generator. Thus the additive map commutes with
1308	  the \(R'\)-action on generators, hence everywhere by bilinearity; together with the
1309	  bridge \(eT\) this promotes the standalone equivalence to the desired bundled
1310	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism. Being a composite of linear equivalences the result is an
1311	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of its constituents carries a flatness hypothesis.
1312	\end{proof}

=== gr_affine_chart block ===
  \label{def:gr_affine_chart}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.affineChart}
  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
  % sub-section "Construction by gluing together affine patches" (read from
  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L807-L821).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "If $M$ is a $d\times r$-matrix, and
  %  $I\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with cardinality $\#(I)$ equal
  %  to $d$, the $I$ th minor $M_I$ of $M$ will
  %  mean the $d\times d$ minor of $M$ whose columns are indexed by
  %  $I$.
  %
  %  For any subset $I\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(I) =d$,
  %  consider the $d\times r$ matrix $X^I$ whose $I$ the minor
  %  $X^I_I$ is the $d\times d$ identity matrix $1_{d\times d}$, while the remaining
  %  entries of $X^I$ are independent variables $x^I_{p,q}$ over $\Z$.
  %  Let $\Z[X^I]$ denote the polynomial ring in the variables
  %  $x^I_{p,q}$, and let $U^I = \spec \Z[X^I]$, which is non-canonically
  %  isomorphic to the affine space $\AA^{d(r-d)}_{\Z}$."
  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
  Let \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). Let \(X^I\) denote the
  \(d \times r\) matrix whose \(I\)-th minor \(X^I_I\) is the \(d \times d\)
  identity matrix \(1_{d \times d}\) and whose remaining \(d(r - d)\) entries are
  independent indeterminates \(x^I_{p,q}\) (\(1 \le p \le d\),
  \(q \in \{1,\dots,r\} \setminus I\)) over \(\mathbb{Z}\). Write
  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) for the polynomial ring in these \(d(r-d)\) variables and
  set
  \[
    U^I \;:=\; \Spec \mathbb{Z}[X^I].
  \]
  The choice of an ordering of the free entries gives a (non-canonical)
  isomorphism \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\); in particular
  \(U^I\) is a smooth affine \(\mathbb{Z}\)-scheme of relative dimension
  \(d(r-d)\). Intuitively a \(\mathbb{Z}\)-point of \(U^I\) is a \(d \times r\)
  matrix whose \(I\)-columns form the identity, i.e.\ a rank-\(d\) quotient of
  \(\mathbb{Z}^{r}\) trivialised on the coordinates \(I\).
\end{definition}

import Mathlib

/-!
# The Quot scheme: Quot-foundations layer

This file packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction. It introduces the
per-fiber Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, the Quot functor
`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `T`-flat coherent quotients, and the Grassmannian
*scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a linear-algebra
Grassmannian) together with its representability.

The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:

1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def) — the
   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.

2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def) — the **Quot
   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.

# Project Progress

## Current Stage

prover

## Stages
- [x] init
- [x] autoformalize
- [ ] prover
- [ ] polish

## End-state overview

**Zero inline `sorry` in the dependency cone of the seven seed declarations + kernel-only
axioms.** Čech-independent leg of the parent's `thm:fga_pic_representability` cone: flat base
change (i=0), generic flatness, and the Quot foundations. Full arc in STRATEGY.md.

## Iter-007 disposition (effort-break + open third lane)

progress-critic `iter007`: **CHURNING on both FBC-A and GF-alg** (FBC at higher severity — the
blueprint carried a provably-wrong `map_smul'` prescription; GF at the STUCK boundary). The named
corrective for both is **blueprint decomposition of the Mathlib-absent cruxes into concretely-typed
sub-lemmas** — NOT another prover round on the monoliths. Deployed THIS iter:

- **FBC** (`Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`): rewrote the unsound `lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv`
  proof (eT-bridge rationale + generator-wise R'-linearity); split `lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace_eq`
  into `lem:base_change_mate_unit_value` / `…_fstar_reindex` / `…_gstar_transpose` + thin assembly.
- **GF** (`Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`): added `lem:gf_generic_rank_ses`, `lem:gf_torsion_reindex`,
  `lem:gf_clear_one_denominator` with concrete Lean signatures (mathlib-analogist verified the API);
  rewrote L5/L4 as thin assemblies. **Key finding: the L5 induction must generalize the base domain
  `A` (not just `N`)** — the real cause of the iter-006 stall (recipe: `analogies/gf-generic-rank-ses.md`).

blueprint-reviewer `iter007`: **HARD GATE PASS** for all three lanes — FBC + GF sub-lemma chains are
complete + correct (iter-006 must-fix RESOLVED), QUOT-defs frontier cleared. iter-008 MUST dispatch
the FBC and GF provers (the critic warns a third plan-only iter on these routes = avoidance).

**FBC-A and GF-alg are intentionally DEFERRED from prover dispatch this iter** (blueprint was being
decomposed; dispatching the monolith again is the churn the critic exists to prevent). This iter's
prover budget goes to the gate-cleared QUOT-defs frontier (genuine parallelism, critic-approved).

## Current Objectives

The QUOT-defs frontier (leandag-ready, blueprint complete + correct) opens as the third parallel
lane. Both files import only Mathlib (QuotScheme already wired; GrassmannianCells wired this iter by
refactor `scaffold-grcells`, build GREEN with the `affineChart` stub). Build is GREEN entering.

1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean`** — Build the three QUOT-defs predicate/helper
   definitions that do NOT yet exist in the file (the 4 existing typed-sorry stubs — `hilbertPolynomial`,
   `QuotFunctor`, `Grassmannian`, `Grassmannian.representable` — are BLOCKED QUOT-repr/SNAP; leave them
   untouched). Build, bottom-up and axiom-clean, from the blueprint chapter:
   - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator` (`def:modules_annihilator`) — the annihilator ideal
     sheaf `Ann_{O_X(U)}(F(U))` of a sheaf of modules on affine opens. Archon-original primitive.
   - `AlgebraicGeometry.SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank` (`def:is_locally_free_of_rank`) — the
     rank-`d` local-freeness predicate (locally trivialised by `O_U^d`). Source: Nitsure §1 Ex.(2).
   - `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedRing` (`def:sectionGradedRing`) — the graded ring
     `⊕_{m≥0} Γ(X_s, L_s^⊗m)`. Source: Nitsure §1.
   Blueprint: `chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex` (`def:modules_annihilator`, `def:is_locally_free_of_rank`,
   `def:sectionGradedRing`). [prover-mode: mathlib-build]

2. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianCells.lean`** — Fill the `affineChart` sorry (line ~60).
   Construct `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.affineChart (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) : Scheme` as
   `Spec` of the polynomial ring `ℤ[X^I]` on the `d(r-d)` free matrix entries, i.e.
   `Spec (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ))` (or an equivalent index of
   cardinality `d(r-d)`). The `I`-minor of `X^I` is the identity; the other entries are the free
   indeterminates. `U^I ≅ 𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`. Source: Nitsure §1, "Construction by gluing affine patches".
   Blueprint: `chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex` (`def:gr_affine_chart`). The file carries an
   in-code `/- Planner note: -/` with the construction. (default `prove` mode — the stub + recipe exist.)

Mode rationale: QuotScheme builds 3 NEW defs from the chapter bottom-up → `mathlib-build`.
GrassmannianCells fills a single stub whose recipe is pinned → default `prove`. Both lanes are
definition-construction (lower risk than the deep FBC/GF proofs), run independently, import only Mathlib.

## Next-iter ramp (iter-008 — MANDATORY FBC + GF prover dispatch)

per progress-critic `iter007`: a third consecutive plan-only iter on FBC/GF would be avoidance.
With the sub-lemma chains blueprinted + gate-cleared this iter, iter-008 dispatches:

- **FBC-A** (`Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean`): build + prove the 3 new sub-lemmas
  `base_change_mate_unit_value`, `…_fstar_reindex`, `…_gstar_transpose` (small, well-typed), then the
  thin `generator_trace_eq` assembly; and close `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv` `map_smul'` via the
  route-(a) `TensorProduct.ext`-at-the-full-carrier reduction (the in-code recipe + the corrected
  blueprint rationale). If route (a) still fights the opaque instance, dispatch a writer to add the
  small ModuleCat base-change-square helper (route (b)) — flagged by blueprint-writer `fbc-decomp`.
- **GF-alg** (`Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean`): FIRST restructure L5's `Nat.strong_induction_on`
  to generalize the base domain `A` (the load-bearing fix); build + prove `gf_generic_rank_ses` from
  the verified atoms, then `gf_torsion_reindex` (+ the shared variable-drop engine, also serving L4
  `gf_clear_one_denominator`), then the thin L5 assembly. Recipe + signatures:
  `analogies/gf-generic-rank-ses.md`.
- **QUOT-defs** continues: once the 3 predicate defs land, re-sign the 4 QuotScheme stubs against them;
  build the GrassmannianCells transition/cocycle/glued-scheme chain (`Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`).

## Coverage debt

Blueprint↔Lean correspondence healthy: blueprint-reviewer `iter007` + leandag report 0 broken
`\uses{}`, 0 isolated, 0 `unmatched` (lean-without-blueprint), 0 `gaps` (∞-effort holes). The 6 new
sub-lemma blueprint blocks (3 FBC, 3 GF) are blueprint-without-Lean (expected — the iter-008 provers
create them). The FBC orphan-leaf advisory (sub-lemmas only in proof-level `\uses{}`) was fixed this
iter by adding them to the `generator_trace_eq` statement-level `\uses{}`.

## Standing notes

- **Import architecture:** root aggregator `AlgebraicJacobian.lean` imports each leaf — a NEW leaf
  file is invisible to `lake build` until its import is wired there (provers can't edit the aggregator;
  use a refactor to create+wire new files, as done for `GrassmannianCells.lean` this iter).
  `RegroupHelper.lean` imports only Mathlib; `FlatBaseChange.lean` imports RegroupHelper + Mathlib;
  GF/QuotScheme/GrassmannianCells import only Mathlib — lanes stay parallel.
- **Plan-reliability:** do NOT carry `[verified by prover]` Lean-tactic annotations across Mathlib
  pins — the iter-006 `map_smul'` one-liner annotation was pin-specific and misdirected the prover
  (progress-critic `iter007`). Mathlib lemma-existence tags expire each iter.
- **Merge-back discipline:** never rename kept declarations, labels, or file paths; never add `\leanok`
  by hand (deterministic sync owns it). `archon-protected.yaml` is empty — re-signing the seeds is
  permitted.
- **Deprecation / cosmetic debt (non-blocking, future golf):** ~22 `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.val` sites in
  FBC; stale "What remains" comment misrepresenting `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`;
  `UniversalProperty`/`affine_base_iff` naming drift in RelativeSpec. Dedicated golf pass before the
  next Mathlib pin bump. Do NOT mix into active proof lanes.

# Iter 007 — Plan (Quot-Foundations)

## TL;DR

Effort-break + open-third-lane iter. Processed the iter-006 prover results (both PARTIAL, sorry
unchanged 4/4): FBC's `map_smul'` one-liner was definitively REFUTED as mathematically unsound (the
two A-module tensor carriers are different types, not a reducibility diamond), and `generator_trace_eq`
got only an `ext x` reduction; GF landed the strong-induction-generalizing-N skeleton but the
generic-rank SES dévissage stayed Mathlib-absent. Both lean-vs-blueprint checkers + the iter-006
review converged: **the cruxes are now monolithic Mathlib-absent constructions that need blueprint
decomposition before any further prover dispatch** (FBC carried a live must-fix on the regroupEquiv
prescription; both GF residues under-specified). progress-critic `iter007`: **CHURNING×2** (FBC
higher severity, GF at STUCK boundary), corrective = blueprint expansion, dispatch=OK. strategy-critic
`iter007`: **SOUND** on all three routes.

This iter: (1) dispatched a mathlib-analogist that resolved the generic-rank Lean API AND surfaced the
real root cause of the GF stall — **the induction must generalize the base domain `A`, not just `N`**;
(2) two blueprint-writers decomposed the FBC and GF cruxes into 6 concretely-typed sub-lemmas + fixed
the FBC must-fix; (3) blueprint-clean + full blueprint-reviewer → **HARD GATE PASS** for all three
lanes; (4) refactor created+wired `GrassmannianCells.lean`; (5) opened the QUOT-defs frontier as the
third parallel PROVER lane (2 files) — the only prover work this iter, since FBC/GF are correctly
deferred for decomposition. Build GREEN throughout.

## State at entry

- iter-006 prover: FBC 4→4 (one-liner refuted, `ext x` landed); GF 4→4 (strong-induction skeleton
  landed, dévissage residue remains). Build green.
- Reviews of record (iter-006): lean-auditor + 2 lean-vs-blueprint-checkers — 0 must-fix on the LEAN
  side, but the FBC checker raised 1 **blueprint** must-fix (the regroupEquiv one-liner prescription is
  wrong) + majors on both files' under-specified residues.

## Critic dispositions

- **progress-critic (`iter007`): CHURNING×2, dispatch=OK.** FBC-A escalated — the blueprint carried a
  provably-wrong `map_smul'` prescription (the "[verified by prover]" annotation was pin-specific and
  misdirected the iter-006 prover); sorry net +1 over the window; OVER_BUDGET (5 iters vs 3–5 est).
  GF-alg at the STUCK boundary (generic-rank SES blocker ×3 prover iters; held off STUCK only by the
  iter-006 strong-induction advance). Named corrective for both: **blueprint decomposition into
  concretely-typed sub-lemmas** + "iter-008 MUST dispatch FBC/GF or it crosses into avoidance." The
  QUOT-defs opening was explicitly confirmed as genuine parallelism, not avoidance.
- **strategy-critic (`iter007`): SOUND, 0 CHALLENGE/REJECT.** FBC direct-on-sections IS the canonical
  i=0 skeleton (Stacks 02KH part 2); GF Nitsure §4 dévissage is the textbook decomposition; QUOT graded-
  Hilbert encoding is sound + Čech-independent for this leg; 3 parallel ACTIVE lanes is correct, not
  over-extension. Two housekeeping must-fixes (non-blocking): format DRIFTED (iter-003 narrative) and
  confirm the re-signed signatures match the parent cone. Good find: `Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Equalizer`
  exists → FBC-B less risky than billed.
- **blueprint-reviewer (`iter007`): HARD GATE PASS** for all three lanes — FBC iter-006 must-fix
  RESOLVED, 6 new sub-lemmas well-formed, QUOT-defs frontier cleared. One advisory (fixed this iter):
  the 2 FBC sub-lemmas were proof-level-`\uses` only → added to statement-level `\uses{}`.

## Decision made

### Defer FBC/GF prover, open QUOT-defs as the third lane; decompose the cruxes via blueprint
- **Option chosen:** no prover on FBC/GF this iter (blueprint being decomposed); the 2 gate-cleared
  QUOT-defs frontier files become the iter's prover lanes; both cruxes decomposed into sub-lemma chains
  for iter-008.
- **Why:** both checkers + the progress-critic agree the cruxes are Mathlib-absent monoliths whose
  blueprints needed decomposition (FBC had a live must-fix); re-dispatching the monoliths is exactly the
  churn the critic exists to catch. The QUOT-defs frontier was gate-cleared at iter-006 and is genuine
  idle-capacity parallelism (critic-confirmed). This satisfies the standing PARALLELISM directive
  (third lane, split across two files) AND the standing REFERENCE-DRIVEN directive (every sub-lemma is
  anchored to Nitsure §4 / Stacks "Affine base change", with verbatim quotes).
- **Trade-off:** one plan-only iter on FBC/GF (no sorry closed there), but the alternative — a 3rd
  monolith re-dispatch — burns a prover iter for guaranteed PARTIAL. The cheap signal that would reverse
  this: if iter-008's sub-lemma stubs turn out un-closeable, GF reclassifies STUCK and pivots.
- **iter-008 commitment:** FBC + GF prover dispatch is MANDATORY (recorded in PROGRESS + task_pending).

### GF base-domain generalization (strategy-modifying, accepted)
The mathlib-analogist found the iter-006 "generalize N" skeleton is necessary but INSUFFICIENT: the
reindex inverts `g ∈ A`, so `T_g` is finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A_g` — base ring `A_g`, not
`A` — and `IH m' _ T_g` doesn't typecheck unless the induction also reverts `A` into the motive. This
is the real cause of the iter-006 stall (not the rank API). Recorded in STRATEGY.md (GF route) + the
blueprint `% LEAN PROOF STRUCTURE` note + `analogies/gf-generic-rank-ses.md`. It is a reversion change,
not a public-signature change.

## Subagents dispatched (this iter)

- progress-critic `iter007` (read-only) — CHURNING×2.
- strategy-critic `iter007` (read-only) — SOUND.
- mathlib-analogist `gf-genrank` (api-alignment) — resolved generic-rank API + base-domain finding;
  persistent `analogies/gf-generic-rank-ses.md`.
- blueprint-writer `fbc-decomp` — fixed regroupEquiv must-fix + 3 FBC sub-lemmas + thin assembly.
- blueprint-writer `gf-decomp` — 3 GF sub-lemmas + L5/L4 thin assemblies + base-domain note.
- blueprint-clean `iter007` — stripped Lean-tactic prose from the FBC regroupEquiv proof (recipe
  preserved in-code + writer report); GF prose already clean.
- blueprint-reviewer `iter007` — HARD GATE PASS, all three lanes.
- refactor `scaffold-grcells` — created+wired `Picard/GrassmannianCells.lean` (affineChart stub, build
  green +1 sorry).

## Subagent skips

- (none material) — all three highly-recommended plan-phase critics ran (progress-critic,
  strategy-critic, blueprint-reviewer). strategy-critic was run rather than skipped because the last
  COMPLETED strategy-critic verdict was iter-003 (iter-005's directive produced no report), so the
  "prior verdict SOUND" skip precondition could not be established, and this iter makes a real
  resource-allocation shift (opening the third lane).

## Notes / risks forward

- FBC route (b) fallback: if the iter-008 prover's route-(a) `TensorProduct.ext`-at-full-carrier still
  fights the opaque `Module R'` instance, dispatch a writer to add the small ModuleCat base-change-square
  helper block (writer `fbc-decomp` is primed to author it).
- GF STUCK risk: GF is at the STUCK boundary; the iter-008 sub-lemmas MUST produce closeable
  obligations. The shared variable-drop engine (L4 ↔ reindex) is the one genuinely novel Mathlib-absent
  build — if it stalls, effort-break it sentence-by-sentence (fine granularity).
- Blueprint-clean removed the Lean lemma-name recipe from the FBC regroupEquiv visible prose (purity);
  it survives in the `.lean` in-code comment at the sorry + the writer report — the iter-008 prover
  reads its own file, so the recipe is available.
